skill.name: nodejs-best-practices
skill.description: Node.js development principles and decision-making. Framework selection, async patterns, security, and architecture. Teaches thinking, not copying.
skill.summary: Guides Node.js architecture, framework choices, async patterns, validation, security, and testing decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command, filesystem, credential, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found only Markdown guidance, diagrams, HTTP status references, and safe security checklists; no executable code, prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences and Inline Terms Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown code fences, decision diagrams, and inline JavaScript terms such as async/await and Promise.all. The skill does not grant shell tools and does not contain executable command instructions at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown formatting, diagrams, or inert code terminology. No shell execution tool is allowed by the skill and no command runner is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Filesystem Warning Is Advisory Text
low_findings.description: The filesystem alert is a best-practice warning that says to avoid synchronous filesystem methods in production. It does not ask the assistant to read, write, or exfiltrate files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a defensive recommendation about event loop blocking. It names fs.readFileSync only as an anti-pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Credential Term Appears in Validation Checklist
low_findings.description: The credential-file alert maps to the word Cookies in a security mindset checklist. The text recommends validation and does not reference browser storage paths or credential extraction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term appears in a list of inputs to validate. There is no file path, browser profile reference, or credential access instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert From Substring Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to descriptive prose in the front matter and closing reminder. The skill does not recommend DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain ordinary description text and the word deserves. No cryptographic implementation or recommendation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: HTTP Status and Heading Text Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to HTTP status code explanations and a section heading for anti-patterns. These lines do not gather system information or instruct environment probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text explains API responses and labels a documentation section. There is no command, host enumeration, or system discovery workflow.

content.value_statement: Node.js projects often fail when teams copy patterns without matching them to deployment, scale, and team needs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical framework, architecture, validation, security, and testing decisions.
actual_capabilities: Compares Hono, Fastify, Express, NestJS, Next.js API routes, and tRPC by project context.
Guides choices for Node.js runtime features, native TypeScript, ESM, CommonJS, Bun, and Deno.
Explains layered backend architecture with controller, service, and repository responsibilities.
Recommends error handling, validation, and security checks for API projects.
Helps prioritize unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for Node.js applications.
limitations: It is a guidance document, not an automated linter or scanner.
It does not include runnable templates, package files, or project scaffolding.
Some recommendations may need updates as Node.js and framework releases change.
It cannot replace a security review of production code and infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Select a Backend Framework
use_cases.description: Compare Node.js framework options against deployment target, team skill, performance needs, and ecosystem requirements.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers starting a new API

use_cases.title: Review Service Architecture
use_cases.description: Check whether routes, business logic, data access, validation, and error handling have clear responsibilities.
use_cases.target_user: Tech leads reviewing a growing codebase

use_cases.title: Plan Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Create a practical checklist for validation, secrets handling, rate limiting, dependency audits, and test coverage.
use_cases.target_user: Startup teams preparing a Node.js launch

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Node.js framework for this project. Ask about deployment target, team experience, performance needs, and existing code before recommending options.

prompt_templates.title: Improve API Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Node.js API structure against layered architecture principles. Identify where controllers, services, repositories, validation, and errors should live.

prompt_templates.title: Design Error and Validation Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an error handling and validation strategy for a production Node.js API. Include boundary validation, safe client responses, logging context, and failure modes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Node.js Delivery Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this Node.js release plan for framework fit, async patterns, event loop risks, security controls, and test priorities. Give concrete changes before launch.

output_examples.input: I need a fast API for a serverless edge deployment.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare edge cold starts, framework size, TypeScript support, and middleware needs, then recommend Hono if the constraints fit.

output_examples.input: My routes contain validation, database calls, and payment logic.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest moving business rules into services, data access into repositories, and validation to API boundaries.

output_examples.input: We are preparing a Node.js API for production.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a checklist covering input validation, parameterized queries, password hashing, JWT checks, rate limiting, headers, secrets, and dependency audits.

best_practices: Ask for deployment target, team experience, and growth expectations before choosing a framework.
Keep validation at system boundaries and avoid leaking internal details in client errors.
Use tests to protect critical paths, integration points, and important failure behavior.
anti_patterns: Choosing Express, Fastify, Hono, or NestJS by habit instead of project context.
Putting business logic, validation, and database access directly inside route handlers.
Using synchronous filesystem work or CPU-heavy operations on the event loop in production.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full Node.js application?
faq.answer: No. It provides decision guidance and review criteria for building or improving Node.js applications.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it discuss?
faq.answer: It discusses Hono, Fastify, Express, NestJS, Next.js API routes, and tRPC.

faq.question: Can it help with security planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers validation, password hashing, JWT checks, rate limiting, security headers, HTTPS, CORS, secrets, and dependency audits.

faq.question: Is it useful for existing legacy projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Express, CommonJS, legacy constraints, and gradual architectural improvement.

faq.question: Does it run commands or inspect local files by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide and only allows read and edit style tools.

faq.question: How should teams use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Use it to ask better planning questions, review architecture choices, and create focused implementation checklists.
